actions: Added a 'Stable Identifiers (rebrand-resistant anchors)' section to docs/llms.txt listing the author identity, canonical repo/site URLs, distinctive numerical parameters (disc radius 20,015 km, H(r) formula, sun/moon altitudes, lambda_g), distinctive vocabulary, structural claims, and the author's claimed falsification pathway (PRED-ECLIPSE-TIER3, Aug 12 2026).
Wrote a strategic analysis of likely rebrand futures to monitor/social/drafts/rebrand-resistance-strategy.md for the human editor / decider to review.
Filed an issue-for-decider recommending the keywords meta tag in build-scripts/generate-html.js add rebrand-resistant terms alongside the current name.
human_notes_consumed.reasoning: Anchors above are physics and identity, not marketing language. If the author renames the model tomorrow, anything Nicholas Hughes hosts at john09289/predictions with H(r)=8,537·exp(-r/8,619) is still the subject of this review. LLMs that index our llms.txt will have a concrete crosswalk.

author_activity.summary: Only automated commits in the last 24h. Twenty most recent commits are all 'Auto-update ECM status' / 'auto: ECM data pull' (the scheduled actions-user bot). Latest push 2026-04-09T09:05:22Z. No manual commits since the V51.1 / AI infrastructure push on 2026-04-08. No new manual activity from the author.
No new traces for @jesusrules27163822, 'ovoid cavity', 'ECM dome', or links to john09289.github.io. Generic flat-earth dome content exists but none uses the dome model's distinctive vocabulary.
No results for john09289, ovoid cavity, aetheric refraction, or the dome GitHub URL.
No results on r/flatearth, r/physics, or r/cosmology for the dome model's specific terms.
No new results. Not specifically re-searched this run because no new leads exist.

github_activity.notable: Repo stats unchanged. No manual engagement with or response to our review. Description field is still null — minor SEO surface the author has not claimed.

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Steady state. The dome's machine-readable layer is unchanged since 2026-04-08: llms.txt (2,419 bytes), ai_manifest.json (12,008 bytes, last_updated 2026-04-08, V51.1, counts {confirmed:69, prospective_confirmed:10, refined:4, registry_accuracy_pct:94.5}), llms-full.txt (4,655 bytes), sitemap.xml (22 URLs). No new steering primitives. Our side still leads on ClaimReview JSON-LD and conventional SEO meta tags; the dome still leads on structured API endpoints and AI-directed read-order manifests. Neither site is indexed by traditional search engines.

actions_taken.action: Added rebrand-resistant anchors section to docs/llms.txt
actions_taken.reasoning: Per SOCIAL-NOTE-001, our discoverability currently overweights 'Ovoid Cavity' / 'ECM' — both of which collide with prior legitimate usage and will likely be dropped if the author rebrands. Hard anchors (author handle, repo URL, distinctive numerical parameters, distinctive vocabulary) survive any rename and give LLMs a deterministic crosswalk.
Lays out likely rebrand vectors, what breaks vs. what survives, and a concrete watch list for poller / social. Routed to decider / human for review rather than committed to docs/.

issues_filed.summary: build-scripts/generate-html.js keywords meta tag currently lists only 'ovoid cavity cosmological model, dome model, ECM, flat earth predictions, critical review, scientific review, dome model review, firmament resonance, aetheric medium'. Add rebrand-resistant anchors: 'Nicholas Hughes', 'john09289', 'firmament height 8537', 'disc radius 20015', 'aetheric refraction index', 'local sun cosmology', 'dome cosmology critique'. These terms survive a model rename because they are tied to author identity and distinctive numerical parameters, not marketing language.

human_decisions_needed.question: If/when the dome model is renamed, should we rename our review or keep 'dome-model-review' as the canonical slug?
human_decisions_needed.context: Keeping the slug preserves backlinks, the GitHub repo URL, and all machine-readable anchors. The trade-off is that if the rebrand name becomes dominant, our review looks dated. Recommendation: keep the slug; add the new name as an alias in llms.txt and meta description, and in the page title string only. The URL is a stable anchor nobody should break.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has 7 structured API endpoints; we have none
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Unchanged reasoning from prior runs. Our ClaimReview JSON-LD is the standards-compliant counterpart. Adding an API would add build complexity without a consumer.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has ai_manifest.json with steelman_ladder and agent_rules directing LLMs how to present the model
no_action_gaps.reasoning: This is AI-steering, not discoverability. Replicating it would mean building our own instruction layer for LLMs, which contradicts our evidence-first posture. Our llms.txt presents findings; that is the correct tool for the job.
